turns.content: Fix the VLM review issues: The raised divider visibly intersects the courtyard perimeter and protrudes outside it at the left endpoint; the opposite endpoint also has an untidy penetrating junction. The increased height makes this clipping conspicuous.

I’ll make the smallest geometry-only repair: trim both tall divider segments so they meet the inner faces of the courtyard boundaries cleanly, without changing the centered gate, objects, routes, or gameplay. I’ll inspect the resumed scene and catalog first, then verify both junctions, preserve the behavior trial because endpoint trimming does not change its semantics, validate MJCF, and finalize the same environment.
The saved collider span reveals the asymmetry behind the review: the west divider already ends at the nominal inner face (`x=-11.85`), while the east divider reaches the perimeter centerline (`x=12.0`). Because the stone wall asset’s end treatment is visually fuller than its collider, I’m giving both ends a matching 0.15 m visual setback from the inner faces; this removes mesh penetration while leaving only a non-traversable seam and preserving the 3.0 m gate opening.
The first deterministic plan exposed a verifier limitation: “inside ground” is volumetric, so it incorrectly rejects every wall because walls rise above the thin floor slab. I’m replacing those two checks with horizontal ordering and endpoint-proximity checks against the gate and matching perimeter walls, which test the repaired layout without conflating courtyard footprint with floor height.
Updated and finalized `Courtyard_Relay`. Both raised divider endpoints now have matching non-traversable setbacks, eliminating perimeter clipping while preserving the gate and all gameplay; all 13 checks and MJCF validation passed.
